<!-- <link rel='stylesheet' id='cmtooltip-css' href='https://mnky.agency/wp-content/plugins/enhanced-tooltipglossary/assets/css/tooltip.min.css?ver=4.5.3' type='text/css' media='all' /> -->
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="//mnky.agency/wp-content/cache/wpfc-minified/kom7a0xc/fqru0.css" media="all">
<style id="cmtooltip-inline-css" type="text/css">
#tt {
				z-index: 1500;
		}
		#tt_overlay {
				z-index: 1499;
		}
					#tt #ttcont div.glossaryItemTitle {
			color: #000000 !important;
			}
		
					#tt #ttcont div.glossaryItemTitle {
			background-color: #transparent !important;
			padding: 0 !important;
			margin: 0px !important;
			border-top: 10px solid transparent;
			border-bottom: 10px solid transparent;
			}
		
					#tt #ttcont div.glossaryItemTitle {
			font-size: 13px !important;
			}
		
		#tt #ttcont div.glossaryItemBody {
		padding: 0;
					font-size: 13px;
				}
		#tt #ttcont .mobile-link{
					font-size: 13px;
				}

		.mobile-link a.glossaryLink {
		color: #fff !important;
		}
		.mobile-link:before{content: "Term link:  "}

		
		
		.tiles ul.glossaryList li {
		min-width: 85px !important;
		width:85px !important;
		}
		.tiles ul.glossaryList span { min-width:85px; width:85px;  }
		.cm-glossary.tiles.big ul.glossaryList a { min-width:179px; width:179px }
		.cm-glossary.tiles.big ul.glossaryList span { min-width:179px; width:179px; }

				span.glossaryLink, a.glossaryLink {
		border-bottom: dotted 1px #dd0000;					color: #dd0000 !important;
				}
		span.glossaryLink.temporary, a.glossaryLink.temporary {
		border-bottom: dotted 1px #eeff11;		}
		span.glossaryLink:hover, a.glossaryLink:hover {
		border-bottom: solid 1px #000000;					color:#333333 !important;
				}

				.glossaryList .glossary-link-title {
		font-weight: normal !important;
		}

		
					#tt #tt-btn-close{ color: #222 !important}
		
		.cm-glossary.grid ul.glossaryList li[class^='ln']  { width: 200px !important}

					#tt #tt-btn-close{
			direction: rtl;
			font-size: 20px !important
			}
		
		
					#tt #ttcont glossaryItemBody * {color: #ffffff}
		

		
					#tt #ttcont a{color: #2EA3F2 !important}
		
		
		
					#ttcont {
			box-shadow: 0px 0px 20px #666666;
			-moz-box-shadow: 0px 0px 20px #666666;
			-webkit-box-shadow: 0px 0px 20px #666666;
			}
					.cm-glossary.term-carousel .slick-slide,
			.cm-glossary.tiles-with-definition ul > li { height: 250px !important}
					.cm-glossary.tiles-with-definition ul {
			grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, 220px) !important;
			}
		
		.glossary-search-wrapper {
		display: inline-block;
				}


		
		input.glossary-search-term {
				outline: none;
																}


		
		
		button.glossary-search.button {
		outline: none;
												}
/*# sourceURL=cmtooltip-inline-css */
</style>

/* #Typography */body {font-style: normal;font-weight: 300;font-size: 16px;line-height: 1.6;font-family: 'Open Sans', sans-serif;letter-spacing: 0px;text-align: left;color: #000000;}h1,.h1-style {font-style: normal;font-weight: 400;font-size: 24px;line-height: 1.4;font-family: Hind, sans-serif;letter-spacing: 0px;text-align: inherit;color: #000000;}h2,.h2-style {font-style: a:hover,h2 a:hover,h3 a:hover,h4 a:hover,h5 a:hover,h6 a:hover { color: #dd0000; }a:hover { color: #000000; }blockquote {color: #dd0000;}/* #Header */.site-header__wrap {background-color: #ffffff;background-repeat: repeat;background-position: center top;background-attachment: scroll;;}/* ##Top Panel */.top-panel {color: #000000;background-color: #ffffff;}/* #Main Menu */.main-navigation {font-style: normal;font-weight: 400;font-size: 14px;line-height: 1.4;font-family: Hind, sans-serif;letter-spacing: 0px;}.main-navigation a,.menu-item-has-children:before {color: }:-ms-input-placeholder{ color: #000000; }/* #Entry Meta */.posted-on,.cat-links,.byline,.tags-links {color: #000000;}.comments-button {color: #000000;}.comments-button:hover {color: rgb(255,38,38);}.sticky-label {color: #ffffff;background-color: #dd0000;}/* Posts List Item Invert */.invert-hover.has-post-thumbnail:hover,.invert-hover.has-post-thumbnail:hover [{"@context":"https:\/\/schema.org\/","@type":"VideoObject","@id":"https:\/\/mnky.agency\/real-estate-landing-page-guide\/#videoobject","url":"https:\/\/mnky.agency\/real-estate-landing-page-guide\/","headline":"Real Estate Landing Pages: The Ultimate Lead Generation Guide","datePublished":"2025-07-16T05:55:00-07:00","dateModified":"2025-09-01T01:39:25-07:00","description":"Discover how to build real estate landing pages that convert. Proven strategies, niche tips, and case studies from MNKY.agency CEO Stu Hill.","transcript":"https:\/\/youtu.be\/d1ptyroJess     Real Estate Landing Pages That Convert: The Ultimate Guide for Agents & Brokerages    Author: J. Stuart Hill, CEO of MNKY.agencyEstimated Read Time: 35\u201340 minutes    \ud83e\udde0 TL;DR (Too Long; Didn\u2019t Read)    If you\u2019re a real estate agent, team leader, or brokerage owner looking to generate more leads online, this guide is your blueprint. I\u2019ve spent over two decades building real estate websites and lead generation systems for every niche imaginable \u2014 from probate and trustee sales to luxury listings and first-time homebuyers. And if there\u2019s one thing I\u2019ve learned, it\u2019s this:     Landing pages are the single most underutilized tool in real estate marketing.     In this guide, I\u2019ll walk you through everything you need to know about real estate landing pages \u2014 what they are, why they work, how to build them, and how to get them in front of the right people. Whether you\u2019re running Facebook ads, Google PPC, or just want to capture more organic leads, this is the playbook.    \ud83d\udd11 Key Takeaways     Landing pages convert better than websites. Period. They\u2019re focused, distraction-free, and built for one thing: capturing leads.    Every niche needs a different approach. A probate lead is not the same as a luxury buyer. Your landing pages should reflect that.    Design matters. A beautiful, fast, mobile-optimized page builds trust and increases conversions.    Traffic without strategy is wasted. You need the right ad copy, targeting, and follow-up systems to make landing pages work.    MNKY.agency builds landing pages that work. We\u2019ve helped hundreds of agents and teams generate thousands of leads \u2014 and we can do the same for you.     \ud83d\udc4b About the Author: Stu Hill, CEO of MNKY.agency    I\u2019ve been in the real estate marketing game since the early 2000s \u2014 back when IDX websites were cutting-edge and Craigslist was a lead source. Over the years, I\u2019ve worked with agents in every niche you can think of:     Probate & inherited property    Trustee sales & short sales    Fix & flip investors    Luxury property marketing    First-time homebuyers    New construction & developments     I founded MNKY.agency to bring high-performance marketing to real estate professionals who are serious about growth. We don\u2019t just build pretty websites \u2014 we build systems that generate leads, nurture them, and convert them into closings.    This guide is the culmination of everything I\u2019ve learned about real estate landing pages \u2014 and how to use them to grow your business.    \ud83e\udded What\u2019s Inside This Guide    Table of Contents    \ud83d\udd17 External Resources for Further Reading     HubSpot: What Is a Landing Page?    WordStream: Landing Page Best Practices    NAR: 2024 Real Estate Technology Trends         \ud83d\udcd8 What Is a Real Estate Landing Page?    \ud83c\udfe1 A Landing Page Is Not a Website \u2014 It\u2019s a Conversion Machine    Let\u2019s get one thing straight: a landing page is not your website. It\u2019s not your homepage, your \u201cAbout Us\u201d page, or even your listings page. A landing page is a single, focused page designed with one goal in mind:     To convert visitors into leads.     In the real estate world, that means capturing names, emails, phone numbers \u2014 and sometimes even more detailed information like property preferences, timelines, or financing status.    \ud83e\udde0 The Definition (Without the Jargon)    A real estate landing page is a standalone web page built specifically to:     Promote a single offer (e.g., a home valuation, a list of fixer-uppers, a free buyer\u2019s guide)    Capture lead information through a form    Eliminate distractions (no menus, no outbound links, no fluff)     It\u2019s where you send traffic from your Facebook ads, Google PPC, email campaigns, or even QR codes on yard signs.    \ud83e\udded Why Landing Pages Work So Well in Real Estate    I\u2019ve seen this firsthand over and over again: agents spend thousands driving traffic to their homepage \u2014 and get nothing in return. Why? Because a homepage is like a buffet. It has too many options. People get overwhelmed and bounce.    A landing page, on the other hand, is like a tasting menu. It gives people one clear choice: \u201cWant this? Great. Fill out the form.\u201d    Here\u2019s why they work:     Focused messaging: Tailored to a specific audience or offer    No distractions: No nav bar, no blog links, no rabbit holes    Clear call to action: \u201cGet your free home valuation,\u201d \u201cSee fixer-uppers under $300K,\u201d etc.    Better tracking: You can measure exactly how well each page performs     \ud83e\uddea Real-World Example: The Probate Lead Page That Changed Everything    Years ago, I worked with an agent in Southern California who specialized in probate listings. She was running Google Ads to her homepage and getting zero traction. We built her a simple landing page with a headline that read:     \u201cInheriting a Property? Get a Free Probate Home Valuation Today.\u201d     We added a short form, a testimonial from a past client, and a downloadable probate checklist. Within 30 days, she had 17 qualified leads \u2014 and closed two listings within 90 days.    That\u2019s the power of a landing page.    \ud83e\uddf1 Key Components of a Real Estate Landing Page    We\u2019ll go deeper into this in Section 4, but here\u2019s a quick preview of what every high-performing landing page includes:     Headline: Clear, benefit-driven, and niche-specific    Subheadline: Reinforces the offer    Lead Form: Short and sweet (name, email, maybe phone)    Visuals: Local imagery, property photos, or video    Trust Signals: Testimonials, reviews, badges    Call to Action (CTA): \u201cGet Your Free Report,\u201d \u201cSee Listings Now,\u201d etc.     \ud83d\udd04 Landing Pages by Niche    One of the biggest mistakes I see agents make is using the same landing page for every campaign. That\u2019s like using the same script for a luxury buyer and a first-time investor \u2014 it just doesn\u2019t work.    Here are a few niche-specific landing page ideas:    NicheLanding Page OfferProbateFree probate home valuation + checklistTrustee SalesList of upcoming trustee sales in your areaFix & FlipOff-market deals under $300KFirst-Time BuyersFree guide: \u201cBuying Your First Home in \u201dLuxuryPrivate access to luxury listings not on ZillowRelocation\u201cMoving to ? Get our relocation guide\u201d    Each of these deserves its own landing page \u2014 and its own messaging.    \ud83d\udd17 External Resources     Unbounce: What Is a Landing Page?    Neil Patel: Landing Page vs Website    NAR: Marketing to Real Estate Niches     \ud83e\uddf0 Pro Tip from Stu    \u201cIf you\u2019re sending traffic to your homepage, you\u2019re wasting money. Every ad campaign should have its own landing page \u2014 period.\u201d        \ud83d\ude80 Why Landing Pages Outperform Traditional Real Estate Websites    \ud83e\udde9 The Problem with Most Real Estate Websites    Let me be blunt: most real estate websites are built to look good, not to convert.    They\u2019re packed with features \u2014 IDX search, blog posts, agent bios, testimonials, neighborhood guides \u2014 and while all of that has its place, it\u2019s not what you want when you\u2019re running a lead generation campaign.     A traditional website is like a Swiss Army knife. A landing page is a scalpel.     When someone clicks on your ad or email, they\u2019re not looking to explore your brand. They\u2019re looking for a solution to a specific problem \u2014 and they want it fast.    \ud83e\udde0 The Psychology of Focus    Here\u2019s a quick marketing truth I\u2019ve learned over 20 years:     The more choices you give someone, the less likely they are to choose anything.     This is called the Paradox of Choice, and it\u2019s why landing pages work so well. They remove the clutter, the distractions, and the decision fatigue. They give the visitor one clear path forward.    Compare this:    Traditional WebsiteLanding PageNavigation bar with 6+ linksNo navigationMultiple CTAs (\u201cSearch Homes,\u201d \u201cContact Us,\u201d \u201cJoin Our Team\u201d)One CTA (\u201cGet Your Free Home Valuation\u201d)Competing content (blogs, listings, videos)Singular focusGeneric messagingNiche-specific messagingLower conversion rateHigher conversion rate    \ud83d\udcca Real Numbers: Landing Page vs Website Performance    At MNKY.agency, we\u2019ve run hundreds of A\/B tests comparing traffic sent to a homepage vs. a landing page. Here\u2019s what we typically see:     Homepage conversion rate: 0.5% \u2013 1.5%    Landing page conversion rate: 5% \u2013 15% (sometimes higher with retargeting)     That\u2019s a 10x improvement in some cases \u2014 just by changing where the traffic lands.    \ud83e\uddea Case Study: Fix & Flip Campaign in Phoenix    One of our clients in Phoenix was targeting fix & flip investors. They were sending Facebook traffic to their homepage, which had a blog, IDX search, and a bunch of other content. Conversion rate? 0.9%.    We built a dedicated landing page with the headline:     \u201cGet Access to Off-Market Fixer-Uppers in Phoenix \u2014 Before They Hit Zillow.\u201d     We added a short form, a few photos of distressed properties, and a simple CTA: \u201cSend Me the List.\u201d    Conversion rate jumped to 7.8% \u2014 and the client closed three deals in the first 60 days.    \ud83e\uddf0 Why Landing Pages Are Easier to Optimize    Another huge benefit of landing pages? You can test and tweak them quickly.     Want to try a new headline? Easy.    Want to A\/B test your CTA button? Done.    Want to swap out the lead magnet? No problem.     With a traditional website, you\u2019re often stuck with a rigid CMS or a developer backlog. With landing pages, especially the ones we build at MNKY.agency, you can iterate fast \u2014 and that means better results, faster.    \ud83d\udd17 External Resources     Crazy Egg: Why Landing Pages Convert Better    Instapage: Landing Page vs Homepage    MarketingExperiments: The Power of Focused Messaging     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Insight    \u201cI\u2019ve seen agents double their lead volume overnight \u2014 not by spending more on ads, but by sending traffic to the right page. That\u2019s the power of focus.\u201d    \ud83e\udde9 Types of Real Estate Landing Pages (With Examples)    \ud83c\udfaf One Size Doesn\u2019t Fit All    If there\u2019s one thing I\u2019ve learned after working with thousands of agents and teams, it\u2019s this:     Every real estate niche needs its own landing page strategy.     A first-time buyer doesn\u2019t want the same information as a luxury seller. An investor looking for off-market deals doesn\u2019t care about your open house schedule. That\u2019s why we don\u2019t build cookie-cutter pages at MNKY.agency \u2014 we build niche-specific landing pages that speak directly to your ideal client.    Let\u2019s break down the most effective types of real estate landing pages, complete with examples and use cases.    \ud83c\udfe0 1. Home Valuation Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     Homeowners thinking about selling    Curious neighbors    Probate or inherited property owners     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cFind Out What Your Home Is Worth \u2014 Instantly.\u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     Address input field    Instant or delayed valuation    Optional email capture for full report    Local imagery and trust signals     \u2705 Why It Works:    This is one of the highest-converting landing page types because it taps into curiosity and financial motivation. Everyone wants to know what their home is worth \u2014 especially in a shifting market.    \ud83e\udde0 Pro Tip:    Use dynamic content to personalize the page based on ZIP code or neighborhood.    \ud83e\uddfe 2. Seller Lead Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     Homeowners ready to list    FSBOs    Expired listings     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cGet a Free Market Report & Custom Listing Strategy\u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     Short form (name, email, address)    Testimonials from past sellers    Timeline selector (\u201cThinking of selling in 30\/60\/90 days?\u201d)    CTA: \u201cRequest My Seller Strategy\u201d     \u2705 Why It Works:    This page positions you as a local expert and gives sellers a reason to reach out before they list.    \ud83c\udfd8\ufe0f 3. Buyer Lead Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     First-time buyers    Relocating families    Upsizers\/downsizers     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cGet Access to Homes in  Before They Hit Zillow\u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     Property type selector (condo, single-family, etc.)    Budget range    Email capture    CTA: \u201cSend Me the List\u201d     \u2705 Why It Works:    This taps into FOMO (fear of missing out) and gives buyers a reason to opt in for exclusive access.    \ud83d\udee0\ufe0f 4. Investor Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     Fix & flip investors    Buy-and-hold landlords    Out-of-state investors     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cGet Weekly Off-Market Deals in \u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     Property type (distressed, multi-family, etc.)    ROI calculator or sample deal    CTA: \u201cSend Me Investment Deals\u201d     \u2705 Why It Works:    Investors are data-driven \u2014 give them numbers, not fluff. Include cap rates, ARV, and cash flow projections if possible.    \ud83c\udfdb\ufe0f 5. Probate & Inherited Property Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     Heirs and executors    Attorneys    Families managing estates     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cFree Probate Home Valuation + Step-by-Step Guide\u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     Sensitive, empathetic language    Downloadable probate checklist    CTA: \u201cGet My Free Guide\u201d     \u2705 Why It Works:    This niche requires trust and empathy. The right tone and resources can make all the difference.    \ud83c\udfd9\ufe0f 6. Luxury Property Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     High-net-worth individuals    Relocation clients    International buyers     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cPrivate Access to Luxury Listings in \u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     High-end visuals and branding    Discreet contact form    CTA: \u201cRequest Private Access\u201d     \u2705 Why It Works:    Luxury buyers expect exclusivity and discretion. Your landing page should reflect that.    \ud83e\uddf3 7. Relocation Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     Out-of-town buyers    Corporate relocations    Military families     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cFree Relocation Guide to \u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     Downloadable PDF    School district info    Neighborhood highlights    CTA: \u201cSend Me the Guide\u201d     \u2705 Why It Works:    Relocation clients are information-hungry. Give them a reason to trust you before they even arrive.    \ud83e\uddea Bonus: Event-Based Landing Pages     Open House Registration    Webinar Signups    Virtual Tours    First-Time Buyer Workshops     These are great for short-term campaigns and can be repurposed for retargeting.    \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Insight     \u201cThe more specific your landing page, the better it converts. Don\u2019t try to be everything to everyone. Speak directly to one person, one problem, one solution.\u201d     \ud83d\udd17 External Resources    Leadpages: Real Estate Landing Page Templates     ClickFunnels: Real Estate Funnel Examples    Placester: Real Estate Lead Capture Ideas             \ud83e\uddf1 Anatomy of a High-Converting Real Estate Landing Page    \ud83c\udfaf The Goal: One Page, One Purpose    Every landing page you build \u2014 whether it\u2019s for probate leads, luxury buyers, or first-time homebuyers \u2014 should be designed with one clear goal in mind:     Get the visitor to take action.     That action might be filling out a form, downloading a guide, or requesting a list of homes. But the key is focus. No distractions. No competing CTAs. Just one clear path forward.    Let\u2019s break down the anatomy of a landing page that actually converts.    \ud83e\uddec 1. The Headline: Your First (and Best) Shot    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Grab attention and communicate value in 5 seconds or less.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Be specific: \u201cGet a Free Home Valuation in \u201d is better than \u201cFind Out What Your Home Is Worth\u201d    Use numbers or timeframes: \u201cSee Fixer-Uppers Under $300K\u201d or \u201cGet Your Report in 60 Seconds\u201d    Speak to the pain point or desire     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cYour headline should answer the question: What\u2019s in it for me? If it doesn\u2019t, rewrite it.\u201d     \ud83e\uddfe 2. The Subheadline: Reinforce the Offer    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Support the headline with a little more detail or urgency.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Clarify what the user will get    Add a benefit or emotional hook    Keep it short \u2014 1\u20132 lines max     Example:\u201cInstant, no-obligation home valuation using real-time market data \u2014 no phone call required.\u201d    \ud83e\uddf0 3. The Lead Capture Form: Less Is More    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Collect just enough information to qualify the lead \u2014 without scaring them off.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Ask for name, email, and maybe phone \u2014 that\u2019s it    Use smart defaults (e.g., autofill ZIP code)    Use a progressive form if you need more info later     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cEvery extra field you add drops your conversion rate. Start simple. You can always qualify later.\u201d     \ud83d\uddbc\ufe0f 4. Visuals: Show, Don\u2019t Just Tell    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Build trust and emotional connection through imagery.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Use local photos \u2014 not stock images    Include a photo of you or your team if possible    Add a short video walkthrough or testimonial if relevant     Bonus: Use a background image of a recognizable neighborhood or skyline to localize the experience.    \ud83d\udee1\ufe0f 5. Trust Signals: Build Credibility Fast    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Reassure the visitor that you\u2019re legit and trustworthy.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Add testimonials from past clients    Include badges (e.g., \u201cTop Producer,\u201d \u201cZillow Premier Agent\u201d)    Show logos of brokerages, MLSs, or media mentions     Example:\u201cAs seen in Realtor.com, Inman, and The Real Deal\u201d    \ud83d\udce3 6. The Call to Action (CTA): Make It Obvious    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Tell the visitor exactly what to do next.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Use action-oriented language: \u201cGet My Report,\u201d \u201cSend Me the List,\u201d \u201cBook My Call\u201d    Make the button stand out with color and contrast    Repeat the CTA at least twice on the page     Bad CTA: \u201cSubmit\u201dGreat CTA: \u201cGet My Free Home Valuation Now\u201d    \ud83e\udded 7. Optional: Scarcity or Urgency    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Encourage immediate action.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Use countdown timers for events or limited offers    Mention limited availability (\u201cOnly 5 spots left for this week\u2019s strategy calls\u201d)    Highlight time-sensitive benefits (\u201cGet your valuation in 60 seconds\u201d)     \ud83e\uddea Real-World Example: Probate Landing Page    Here\u2019s a breakdown of a probate landing page we built for a client in Florida:    ElementExampleHeadline\u201cInheriting a Property? Get a Free Probate Home Valuation\u201dSubheadline\u201cWe\u2019ll help you understand your options \u2014 no pressure, no obligation.\u201dForm FieldsName, Email, Property AddressVisualPhoto of the agent + local courthouseTrust Signal\u201cOver 50 families helped with probate sales in \u201dCTA\u201cGet My Free Valuation\u201d    Conversion rate? 11.2% \u2014 and it generated 3 listings in the first 45 days.    \ud83d\udd17 External Resources     HubSpot: Anatomy of a Landing Page    Unbounce: Landing Page Design Principles    WordStream: 11 Elements of a High-Converting Landing Page     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Insight     \u201cA landing page is like a handshake. It\u2019s your first impression \u2014 and it better be strong, clear, and confident.\u201d         \ud83d\udea6 Traffic Strategies \u2014 How to Drive the Right People to Your Landing Page    \ud83e\udde0 The Truth About Traffic    Here\u2019s something I tell every agent and team we work with at MNKY.agency:     \"You don\u2019t have a lead problem \u2014 you have a traffic problem.\"     If your landing page isn\u2019t converting, it\u2019s usually not the page. It\u2019s the people you\u2019re sending to it. You need qualified, motivated, niche-specific traffic \u2014 and you need to meet them where they are.    Let\u2019s break down the most effective traffic sources for real estate landing pages, and how to use each one strategically.    \ud83d\udcb0 1. Paid Traffic (PPC & Social Ads)    \ud83d\udd25 Why It Works:    Paid traffic is fast, scalable, and highly targeted. You can get leads within hours of launching a campaign \u2014 if your targeting and messaging are dialed in.    \ud83d\udccd Best Platforms:     Facebook & Instagram Ads (great for buyers, sellers, and niche audiences)    Google Search Ads (great for high-intent leads like \u201csell my house fast in \u201d)    YouTube Ads (great for branding + retargeting)     \ud83e\uddf2 Landing Page Pairings:    Traffic SourceBest Landing Page TypeFacebook AdsBuyer, Seller, Probate, RelocationGoogle AdsHome Valuation, Probate, InvestorInstagram AdsLuxury, First-Time BuyerYouTube AdsWebinar, Relocation, Brand Story    \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cDon\u2019t send paid traffic to your homepage. Ever. Every ad should have its own landing page \u2014 and that page should match the ad\u2019s message exactly.\u201d     \ud83d\udd0d 2. Organic SEO    \ud83c\udf31 Why It Works:    SEO takes time, but it\u2019s free, sustainable, and high-converting. People who find you through search are often further down the funnel.    \ud83d\udccd Best Tactics:     Create geo-targeted landing pages (e.g., \u201cHome Valuation in Marbella\u201d)    Optimize for long-tail keywords (e.g., \u201chow to sell an inherited house in Andalusia\u201d)    Use schema markup for local SEO    Build backlinks from local directories and real estate blogs     \ud83e\uddf2 Landing Page Pairings:     Home Valuation    Probate    Relocation    Luxury     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cWe\u2019ve ranked landing pages on page 1 of Google just by optimizing the headline, meta description, and adding 500 words of localized content. SEO doesn\u2019t have to be complicated \u2014 it just has to be consistent.\u201d     \ud83d\udce7 3. Email Marketing    \ud83d\udcec Why It Works:    Email is personal, direct, and high-converting \u2014 especially when paired with a strong offer.    \ud83d\udccd Best Use Cases:     Send a weekly \u201chot list\u201d of properties    Offer a free guide or checklist    Re-engage cold leads with a new offer     \ud83e\uddf2 Landing Page Pairings:     Buyer Lists    Seller Strategy Sessions    Investment Deals    Relocation Guides     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cYour CRM is a goldmine. Don\u2019t just drip on your leads \u2014 give them something new to click on every month.\u201d     \ud83d\udd01 4. Retargeting    \ud83d\udd04 Why It Works:    Most people won\u2019t convert on the first visit. Retargeting brings them back \u2014 and often at a lower cost.    \ud83d\udccd Best Platforms:     Facebook Pixel    Google Display Network    YouTube Retargeting     \ud83e\uddf2 Landing Page Pairings:     All of them \u2014 especially high-ticket niches like luxury or probate     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cIf you\u2019re not retargeting, you\u2019re leaving money on the table. Period.\u201d     \ud83d\udcf1 5. QR Codes & Offline-to-Online    \ud83d\udccd Best Use Cases:     Yard signs    Flyers    Open house materials    Direct mail     \ud83e\uddf2 Landing Page Pairings:     Open House Registration    Home Valuation    Seller Lead Capture     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cWe\u2019ve had clients get 20+ leads from a single open house flyer \u2014 just by adding a QR code that links to a landing page.\u201d     \ud83e\uddea Real-World Example: Facebook Ads + Probate Page    We ran a Facebook campaign for a probate specialist in Texas. The ad copy read:     \u201cInheriting a Property? We\u2019ll Help You Sell It \u2014 Fast, Fair, and Stress-Free.\u201d     We sent traffic to a probate-specific landing page with a free guide and valuation offer. Cost per lead? $6.42. Conversion rate? 9.3%. The agent closed two listings in 60 days.    \ud83d\udd17 External Resources     Meta Ads Library \u2013 See what other agents are running    Google Keyword Planner \u2013 Find high-intent search terms    Mailchimp: Real Estate Email Templates     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Insight     \u201cThe best landing page in the world won\u2019t work without the right traffic. But the right traffic, paired with the right page? That\u2019s where the magic happens.\u201d         \ud83c\udfaf Conversion Optimization \u2014 Getting More Leads from the Same Traffic    \ud83e\udde0 Why Optimization Matters    You\u2019ve built a beautiful landing page. You\u2019re driving traffic. But you\u2019re not getting the leads you expected. Sound familiar?     The truth is, most landing pages don\u2019t need more traffic \u2014 they need better conversion.     At MNKY.agency, we\u2019ve helped clients double or even triple their lead volume without increasing ad spend \u2014 just by optimizing the page itself.    Let\u2019s break down the most effective ways to boost your conversion rate.    \ud83d\udd01 1. A\/B Testing: The Secret Weapon    \ud83e\uddea What It Is:    A\/B testing (also called split testing) is the process of comparing two versions of a landing page to see which one performs better.    \ud83e\uddf2 What to Test:     Headlines: \u201cGet Your Home\u2019s Value\u201d vs. \u201cWhat\u2019s Your Home Worth in Marbella?\u201d    CTA Buttons: \u201cSubmit\u201d vs. \u201cGet My Free Report\u201d    Images: Stock photo vs. local neighborhood photo    Form Fields: 3 fields vs. 5 fields    Trust Signals: With vs. without testimonials     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cAlways be testing. Even a 1% lift in conversion can mean thousands of dollars in extra GCI over time.\u201d     \ud83d\udcf1 2. Mobile Optimization: Non-Negotiable    \ud83d\udcca Why It Matters:    Over 70% of real estate landing page traffic comes from mobile devices. If your page isn\u2019t mobile-friendly, you\u2019re losing leads.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Use large, tappable buttons    Keep forms short and scrollable    Optimize load speed (under 3 seconds)    Avoid pop-ups that block the screen     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cTest your landing page on your own phone. If it\u2019s clunky or slow, fix it \u2014 fast.\u201d     \ud83e\udde0 3. Psychological Triggers That Boost Conversions    \ud83e\uddf2 Social Proof     Add testimonials, reviews, or \u201cX people downloaded this guide\u201d counters    Use real names and photos when possible     \u23f3 Urgency     \u201cOnly 3 spots left for this week\u2019s seller strategy sessions\u201d    Countdown timers for events or limited offers     \ud83d\udd12 Trust & Security     \u201cWe respect your privacy. No spam, ever.\u201d    SSL certificate and secure form messaging     \ud83c\udf81 Reciprocity     Offer something valuable for free (guide, checklist, valuation)    Make it feel exclusive: \u201cOnly available to homeowners in \u201d     \ud83e\uddea Real-World Example: First-Time Buyer Page    We worked with an agent in Atlanta targeting first-time buyers. Her original page had a generic headline, a long form, and no testimonials. Conversion rate? 2.1%    We made three changes:     Changed the headline to: \u201cBuying Your First Home in Atlanta? Start Here.\u201d    Shortened the form to just name + email    Added a testimonial from a recent buyer     New conversion rate? 6.4% \u2014 a 3x improvement with zero extra ad spend.    \ud83d\udcca 4. Use Heatmaps & Analytics    \ud83d\udd0d Tools We Recommend:     Hotjar or Microsoft Clarity for heatmaps and session recordings    Google Analytics for bounce rate and time on page    Meta Pixel and Google Tag Manager for conversion tracking     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cWatch how people actually use your page. Where they scroll, where they click, where they drop off \u2014 it\u2019s like reading their mind.\u201d     \ud83e\uddf0 5. Follow-Up Systems: Don\u2019t Let Leads Go Cold    Even the best landing page won\u2019t close deals on its own. You need a follow-up system that nurtures leads until they\u2019re ready to act.    \ud83d\udd04 Best Practices:     Use an automated email drip (3\u20135 emails minimum)    Send a text within 5 minutes of form submission    Offer a calendar link for booking a call     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cSpeed to lead is everything. If you\u2019re not following up within 5 minutes, someone else is.\u201d     \ud83d\udd17 External Resources     CXL: A\/B Testing Guide    Hotjar: How to Use Heatmaps    NAR: Mobile Usage in Real Estate     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Insight     \u201cConversion optimization isn\u2019t a one-time thing \u2014 it\u2019s a mindset. Test, tweak, repeat. That\u2019s how you turn a good landing page into a lead-generating machine.\u201d         \ud83c\udfaf Real Estate Niches & Customization Tips    \ud83e\udde0 Why Niche-Specific Landing Pages Work    In real estate, context is everything. A probate lead has different needs than a luxury buyer. An investor wants numbers, not fluff. A first-time buyer wants guidance, not jargon.     The more specific your landing page, the more it resonates \u2014 and the better it converts.     At MNKY.agency, we\u2019ve built hundreds of niche-specific landing pages. Here\u2019s how to tailor your messaging, design, and offer for each one.    \ud83c\udfdb\ufe0f 1. Probate & Inherited Property    \ud83c\udfaf Audience:     Heirs, executors, and families managing estates    Often overwhelmed, emotional, and unfamiliar with the process     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:    \u201cFree Probate Home Valuation + Step-by-Step Guide to Selling an Inherited Property\u201d    \ud83e\udde0 Messaging Tips:     Use empathetic, non-salesy language    Focus on education and support    Avoid urgency or pressure tactics     \ud83d\udd27 Customization Ideas:     Include a downloadable probate checklist    Add a short video explaining the probate process    Use testimonials from families you\u2019ve helped     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Insight:     \u201cI\u2019ve worked with dozens of probate specialists. The key is trust. Your landing page should feel like a helping hand, not a sales pitch.\u201d     \ud83c\udfd8\ufe0f 2. First-Time Homebuyers    \ud83c\udfaf Audience:     Millennials, Gen Z, renters, newlyweds    Often anxious, budget-conscious, and unsure where to start     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:    \u201cFree First-Time Buyer Guide + Access to Homes Under  in \u201d    \ud83e\udde0 Messaging Tips:     Be friendly, approachable, and educational    Use simple language \u2014 avoid industry jargon    Highlight affordability and financing options     \ud83d\udd27 Customization Ideas:     Include a mortgage calculator    Offer a downloadable \u201cFirst-Time Buyer Checklist\u201d    Add a FAQ section (\u201cWhat\u2019s a pre-approval?\u201d)     \ud83c\udfd7\ufe0f 3. Fix & Flip \/ Investor Leads    \ud83c\udfaf Audience:     Cash buyers, flippers, landlords    Data-driven, ROI-focused, fast-moving     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:    \u201cGet Weekly Off-Market Deals in  \u2014 Before They Hit the MLS\u201d    \ud83e\udde0 Messaging Tips:     Focus on numbers: ARV, cap rate, ROI    Use urgency and scarcity (\u201cOnly 3 deals left this week\u201d)    Keep it short and direct     \ud83d\udd27 Customization Ideas:     Include a sample deal breakdown    Add a \u201cDeal Analyzer\u201d download    Use a bold, no-nonsense design     \ud83c\udfd9\ufe0f 4. Luxury Buyers & Sellers    \ud83c\udfaf Audience:     High-net-worth individuals, executives, international buyers    Expect discretion, exclusivity, and premium service     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:    \u201cPrivate Access to Luxury Listings in  \u2014 Not Available on Zillow\u201d    \ud83e\udde0 Messaging Tips:     Use elegant, minimalist design    Highlight privacy, exclusivity, and white-glove service    Avoid aggressive CTAs \u2014 use \u201cRequest Access\u201d or \u201cSchedule a Private Consultation\u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Customization Ideas:     Include a video tour of a luxury property    Add a downloadable luxury market report    Use high-end photography and branding     \ud83e\uddf3 5. Relocation Clients    \ud83c\udfaf Audience:     Out-of-town buyers, corporate relocations, military families    Need local knowledge and logistical support     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:    \u201cFree Relocation Guide to  + Neighborhood Insights\u201d    \ud83e\udde0 Messaging Tips:     Position yourself as a local expert and guide    Include school info, commute times, and lifestyle highlights    Use welcoming, helpful language     \ud83d\udd27 Customization Ideas:     Add a downloadable relocation guide    Include a map of neighborhoods with pros\/cons    Offer a virtual consultation booking link     \ud83e\uddfe 6. FSBO & Expired Listings    \ud83c\udfaf Audience:     Sellers who tried to go it alone \u2014 and failed    Frustrated, skeptical, and wary of agents     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:    \u201cWhy Your Home Didn\u2019t Sell \u2014 Free Report + Strategy Session\u201d    \ud83e\udde0 Messaging Tips:     Be empathetic, not critical    Focus on solutions and second chances    Use data to back up your claims     \ud83d\udd27 Customization Ideas:     Include a \u201cTop 5 Reasons Homes Don\u2019t Sell\u201d PDF    Add a testimonial from a former FSBO client    Offer a free pricing analysis     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Insight    \u201cThe riches are in the niches. Proven strategies, niche tips, and case studies from MNKY.agency CEO Stu Hill.","articleBody":"https:\/\/youtu.be\/d1ptyroJess     Real Estate Landing Pages That Convert: The Ultimate Guide for Agents & Brokerages    Author: J. Stuart Hill, CEO of MNKY.agencyEstimated Read Time: 35\u201340 minutes    \ud83e\udde0 TL;DR (Too Long; Didn\u2019t Read)    If you\u2019re a real estate agent, team leader, or brokerage owner looking to generate more leads online, this guide is your blueprint. I\u2019ve spent over two decades building real estate websites and lead generation systems for every niche imaginable \u2014 from probate and trustee sales to luxury listings and first-time homebuyers. And if there\u2019s one thing I\u2019ve learned, it\u2019s this:     Landing pages are the single most underutilized tool in real estate marketing.     In this guide, I\u2019ll walk you through everything you need to know about real estate landing pages \u2014 what they are, why they work, how to build them, and how to get them in front of the right people. Whether you\u2019re running Facebook ads, Google PPC, or just want to capture more organic leads, this is the playbook.    \ud83d\udd11 Key Takeaways     Landing pages convert better than websites. Period. They\u2019re focused, distraction-free, and built for one thing: capturing leads.    Every niche needs a different approach. A probate lead is not the same as a luxury buyer. Your landing pages should reflect that.    Design matters. A beautiful, fast, mobile-optimized page builds trust and increases conversions.    Traffic without strategy is wasted. You need the right ad copy, targeting, and follow-up systems to make landing pages work.    MNKY.agency builds landing pages that work. We\u2019ve helped hundreds of agents and teams generate thousands of leads \u2014 and we can do the same for you.     \ud83d\udc4b About the Author: Stu Hill, CEO of MNKY.agency    I\u2019ve been in the real estate marketing game since the early 2000s \u2014 back when IDX websites were cutting-edge and Craigslist was a lead source. Over the years, I\u2019ve worked with agents in every niche you can think of:     Probate & inherited property    Trustee sales & short sales    Fix & flip investors    Luxury property marketing    First-time homebuyers    New construction & developments     I founded MNKY.agency to bring high-performance marketing to real estate professionals who are serious about growth. We don\u2019t just build pretty websites \u2014 we build systems that generate leads, nurture them, and convert them into closings.    This guide is the culmination of everything I\u2019ve learned about real estate landing pages \u2014 and how to use them to grow your business.    \ud83e\udded What\u2019s Inside This Guide    Table of Contents    \ud83d\udd17 External Resources for Further Reading     HubSpot: What Is a Landing Page?    WordStream: Landing Page Best Practices    NAR: 2024 Real Estate Technology Trends         \ud83d\udcd8 What Is a Real Estate Landing Page?    \ud83c\udfe1 A Landing Page Is Not a Website \u2014 It\u2019s a Conversion Machine    Let\u2019s get one thing straight: a landing page is not your website. It\u2019s not your homepage, your \u201cAbout Us\u201d page, or even your listings page. A landing page is a single, focused page designed with one goal in mind:     To convert visitors into leads.     In the real estate world, that means capturing names, emails, phone numbers \u2014 and sometimes even more detailed information like property preferences, timelines, or financing status.    \ud83e\udde0 The Definition (Without the Jargon)    A real estate landing page is a standalone web page built specifically to:     Promote a single offer (e.g., a home valuation, a list of fixer-uppers, a free buyer\u2019s guide)    Capture lead information through a form    Eliminate distractions (no menus, no outbound links, no fluff)     It\u2019s where you send traffic from your Facebook ads, Google PPC, email campaigns, or even QR codes on yard signs.    \ud83e\udded Why Landing Pages Work So Well in Real Estate    I\u2019ve seen this firsthand over and over again: agents spend thousands driving traffic to their homepage \u2014 and get nothing in return. Why? Because a homepage is like a buffet. It has too many options. People get overwhelmed and bounce.    A landing page, on the other hand, is like a tasting menu. It gives people one clear choice: \u201cWant this? Great. Fill out the form.\u201d    Here\u2019s why they work:     Focused messaging: Tailored to a specific audience or offer    No distractions: No nav bar, no blog links, no rabbit holes    Clear call to action: \u201cGet your free home valuation,\u201d \u201cSee fixer-uppers under $300K,\u201d etc.    Better tracking: You can measure exactly how well each page performs     \ud83e\uddea Real-World Example: The Probate Lead Page That Changed Everything    Years ago, I worked with an agent in Southern California who specialized in probate listings. She was running Google Ads to her homepage and getting zero traction. We built her a simple landing page with a headline that read:     \u201cInheriting a Property? Get a Free Probate Home Valuation Today.\u201d     We added a short form, a testimonial from a past client, and a downloadable probate checklist. Within 30 days, she had 17 qualified leads \u2014 and closed two listings within 90 days.    That\u2019s the power of a landing page.    \ud83e\uddf1 Key Components of a Real Estate Landing Page    We\u2019ll go deeper into this in Section 4, but here\u2019s a quick preview of what every high-performing landing page includes:     Headline: Clear, benefit-driven, and niche-specific    Subheadline: Reinforces the offer    Lead Form: Short and sweet (name, email, maybe phone)    Visuals: Local imagery, property photos, or video    Trust Signals: Testimonials, reviews, badges    Call to Action (CTA): \u201cGet Your Free Report,\u201d \u201cSee Listings Now,\u201d etc.     \ud83d\udd04 Landing Pages by Niche    One of the biggest mistakes I see agents make is using the same landing page for every campaign. That\u2019s like using the same script for a luxury buyer and a first-time investor \u2014 it just doesn\u2019t work.    Here are a few niche-specific landing page ideas:    NicheLanding Page OfferProbateFree probate home valuation + checklistTrustee SalesList of upcoming trustee sales in your areaFix & FlipOff-market deals under $300KFirst-Time BuyersFree guide: \u201cBuying Your First Home in \u201dLuxuryPrivate access to luxury listings not on ZillowRelocation\u201cMoving to ? Get our relocation guide\u201d    Each of these deserves its own landing page \u2014 and its own messaging.    \ud83d\udd17 External Resources     Unbounce: What Is a Landing Page?    Neil Patel: Landing Page vs Website    NAR: Marketing to Real Estate Niches     \ud83e\uddf0 Pro Tip from Stu    \u201cIf you\u2019re sending traffic to your homepage, you\u2019re wasting money. Every ad campaign should have its own landing page \u2014 period.\u201d        \ud83d\ude80 Why Landing Pages Outperform Traditional Real Estate Websites    \ud83e\udde9 The Problem with Most Real Estate Websites    Let me be blunt: most real estate websites are built to look good, not to convert.    They\u2019re packed with features \u2014 IDX search, blog posts, agent bios, testimonials, neighborhood guides \u2014 and while all of that has its place, it\u2019s not what you want when you\u2019re running a lead generation campaign.     A traditional website is like a Swiss Army knife. A landing page is a scalpel.     When someone clicks on your ad or email, they\u2019re not looking to explore your brand. They\u2019re looking for a solution to a specific problem \u2014 and they want it fast.    \ud83e\udde0 The Psychology of Focus    Here\u2019s a quick marketing truth I\u2019ve learned over 20 years:     The more choices you give someone, the less likely they are to choose anything.     This is called the Paradox of Choice, and it\u2019s why landing pages work so well. They remove the clutter, the distractions, and the decision fatigue. They give the visitor one clear path forward.    Compare this:    Traditional WebsiteLanding PageNavigation bar with 6+ linksNo navigationMultiple CTAs (\u201cSearch Homes,\u201d \u201cContact Us,\u201d \u201cJoin Our Team\u201d)One CTA (\u201cGet Your Free Home Valuation\u201d)Competing content (blogs, listings, videos)Singular focusGeneric messagingNiche-specific messagingLower conversion rateHigher conversion rate    \ud83d\udcca Real Numbers: Landing Page vs Website Performance    At MNKY.agency, we\u2019ve run hundreds of A\/B tests comparing traffic sent to a homepage vs. a landing page. Here\u2019s what we typically see:     Homepage conversion rate: 0.5% \u2013 1.5%    Landing page conversion rate: 5% \u2013 15% (sometimes higher with retargeting)     That\u2019s a 10x improvement in some cases \u2014 just by changing where the traffic lands.    \ud83e\uddea Case Study: Fix & Flip Campaign in Phoenix    One of our clients in Phoenix was targeting fix & flip investors. They were sending Facebook traffic to their homepage, which had a blog, IDX search, and a bunch of other content. Conversion rate? 0.9%.    We built a dedicated landing page with the headline:     \u201cGet Access to Off-Market Fixer-Uppers in Phoenix \u2014 Before They Hit Zillow.\u201d     We added a short form, a few photos of distressed properties, and a simple CTA: \u201cSend Me the List.\u201d    Conversion rate jumped to 7.8% \u2014 and the client closed three deals in the first 60 days.    \ud83e\uddf0 Why Landing Pages Are Easier to Optimize    Another huge benefit of landing pages? You can test and tweak them quickly.     Want to try a new headline? Easy.    Want to A\/B test your CTA button? Done.    Want to swap out the lead magnet? No problem.     With a traditional website, you\u2019re often stuck with a rigid CMS or a developer backlog. With landing pages, especially the ones we build at MNKY.agency, you can iterate fast \u2014 and that means better results, faster.    \ud83d\udd17 External Resources     Crazy Egg: Why Landing Pages Convert Better    Instapage: Landing Page vs Homepage    MarketingExperiments: The Power of Focused Messaging     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Insight    \u201cI\u2019ve seen agents double their lead volume overnight \u2014 not by spending more on ads, but by sending traffic to the right page. That\u2019s the power of focus.\u201d    \ud83e\udde9 Types of Real Estate Landing Pages (With Examples)    \ud83c\udfaf One Size Doesn\u2019t Fit All    If there\u2019s one thing I\u2019ve learned after working with thousands of agents and teams, it\u2019s this:     Every real estate niche needs its own landing page strategy.     A first-time buyer doesn\u2019t want the same information as a luxury seller. An investor looking for off-market deals doesn\u2019t care about your open house schedule. That\u2019s why we don\u2019t build cookie-cutter pages at MNKY.agency \u2014 we build niche-specific landing pages that speak directly to your ideal client.    Let\u2019s break down the most effective types of real estate landing pages, complete with examples and use cases.    \ud83c\udfe0 1. Home Valuation Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     Homeowners thinking about selling    Curious neighbors    Probate or inherited property owners     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cFind Out What Your Home Is Worth \u2014 Instantly.\u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     Address input field    Instant or delayed valuation    Optional email capture for full report    Local imagery and trust signals     \u2705 Why It Works:    This is one of the highest-converting landing page types because it taps into curiosity and financial motivation. Everyone wants to know what their home is worth \u2014 especially in a shifting market.    \ud83e\udde0 Pro Tip:    Use dynamic content to personalize the page based on ZIP code or neighborhood.    \ud83e\uddfe 2. Seller Lead Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     Homeowners ready to list    FSBOs    Expired listings     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cGet a Free Market Report & Custom Listing Strategy\u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     Short form (name, email, address)    Testimonials from past sellers    Timeline selector (\u201cThinking of selling in 30\/60\/90 days?\u201d)    CTA: \u201cRequest My Seller Strategy\u201d     \u2705 Why It Works:    This page positions you as a local expert and gives sellers a reason to reach out before they list.    \ud83c\udfd8\ufe0f 3. Buyer Lead Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     First-time buyers    Relocating families    Upsizers\/downsizers     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cGet Access to Homes in  Before They Hit Zillow\u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     Property type selector (condo, single-family, etc.)    Budget range    Email capture    CTA: \u201cSend Me the List\u201d     \u2705 Why It Works:    This taps into FOMO (fear of missing out) and gives buyers a reason to opt in for exclusive access.    \ud83d\udee0\ufe0f 4. Investor Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     Fix & flip investors    Buy-and-hold landlords    Out-of-state investors     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cGet Weekly Off-Market Deals in \u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     Property type (distressed, multi-family, etc.)    ROI calculator or sample deal    CTA: \u201cSend Me Investment Deals\u201d     \u2705 Why It Works:    Investors are data-driven \u2014 give them numbers, not fluff. Include cap rates, ARV, and cash flow projections if possible.    \ud83c\udfdb\ufe0f 5. Probate & Inherited Property Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     Heirs and executors    Attorneys    Families managing estates     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cFree Probate Home Valuation + Step-by-Step Guide\u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     Sensitive, empathetic language    Downloadable probate checklist    CTA: \u201cGet My Free Guide\u201d     \u2705 Why It Works:    This niche requires trust and empathy. The right tone and resources can make all the difference.    \ud83c\udfd9\ufe0f 6. Luxury Property Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     High-net-worth individuals    Relocation clients    International buyers     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cPrivate Access to Luxury Listings in \u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     High-end visuals and branding    Discreet contact form    CTA: \u201cRequest Private Access\u201d     \u2705 Why It Works:    Luxury buyers expect exclusivity and discretion. Your landing page should reflect that.    \ud83e\uddf3 7. Relocation Landing Pages    \ud83c\udfaf Target Audience:     Out-of-town buyers    Corporate relocations    Military families     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:     \u201cFree Relocation Guide to \u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Key Elements:     Downloadable PDF    School district info    Neighborhood highlights    CTA: \u201cSend Me the Guide\u201d     \u2705 Why It Works:    Relocation clients are information-hungry. Give them a reason to trust you before they even arrive.    \ud83e\uddea Bonus: Event-Based Landing Pages     Open House Registration    Webinar Signups    Virtual Tours    First-Time Buyer Workshops     These are great for short-term campaigns and can be repurposed for retargeting.    \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Insight     \u201cThe more specific your landing page, the better it converts. Don\u2019t try to be everything to everyone. Speak directly to one person, one problem, one solution.\u201d     \ud83d\udd17 External Resources    Leadpages: Real Estate Landing Page Templates     ClickFunnels: Real Estate Funnel Examples    Placester: Real Estate Lead Capture Ideas             \ud83e\uddf1 Anatomy of a High-Converting Real Estate Landing Page    \ud83c\udfaf The Goal: One Page, One Purpose    Every landing page you build \u2014 whether it\u2019s for probate leads, luxury buyers, or first-time homebuyers \u2014 should be designed with one clear goal in mind:     Get the visitor to take action.     That action might be filling out a form, downloading a guide, or requesting a list of homes. But the key is focus. No distractions. No competing CTAs. Just one clear path forward.    Let\u2019s break down the anatomy of a landing page that actually converts.    \ud83e\uddec 1. The Headline: Your First (and Best) Shot    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Grab attention and communicate value in 5 seconds or less.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Be specific: \u201cGet a Free Home Valuation in \u201d is better than \u201cFind Out What Your Home Is Worth\u201d    Use numbers or timeframes: \u201cSee Fixer-Uppers Under $300K\u201d or \u201cGet Your Report in 60 Seconds\u201d    Speak to the pain point or desire     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cYour headline should answer the question: What\u2019s in it for me? If it doesn\u2019t, rewrite it.\u201d     \ud83e\uddfe 2. The Subheadline: Reinforce the Offer    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Support the headline with a little more detail or urgency.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Clarify what the user will get    Add a benefit or emotional hook    Keep it short \u2014 1\u20132 lines max     Example:\u201cInstant, no-obligation home valuation using real-time market data \u2014 no phone call required.\u201d    \ud83e\uddf0 3. The Lead Capture Form: Less Is More    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Collect just enough information to qualify the lead \u2014 without scaring them off.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Ask for name, email, and maybe phone \u2014 that\u2019s it    Use smart defaults (e.g., autofill ZIP code)    Use a progressive form if you need more info later     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cEvery extra field you add drops your conversion rate. Start simple. You can always qualify later.\u201d     \ud83d\uddbc\ufe0f 4. Visuals: Show, Don\u2019t Just Tell    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Build trust and emotional connection through imagery.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Use local photos \u2014 not stock images    Include a photo of you or your team if possible    Add a short video walkthrough or testimonial if relevant     Bonus: Use a background image of a recognizable neighborhood or skyline to localize the experience.    \ud83d\udee1\ufe0f 5. Trust Signals: Build Credibility Fast    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Reassure the visitor that you\u2019re legit and trustworthy.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Add testimonials from past clients    Include badges (e.g., \u201cTop Producer,\u201d \u201cZillow Premier Agent\u201d)    Show logos of brokerages, MLSs, or media mentions     Example:\u201cAs seen in Realtor.com, Inman, and The Real Deal\u201d    \ud83d\udce3 6. The Call to Action (CTA): Make It Obvious    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Tell the visitor exactly what to do next.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Use action-oriented language: \u201cGet My Report,\u201d \u201cSend Me the List,\u201d \u201cBook My Call\u201d    Make the button stand out with color and contrast    Repeat the CTA at least twice on the page     Bad CTA: \u201cSubmit\u201dGreat CTA: \u201cGet My Free Home Valuation Now\u201d    \ud83e\udded 7. Optional: Scarcity or Urgency    \ud83d\udccc Purpose:    Encourage immediate action.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Use countdown timers for events or limited offers    Mention limited availability (\u201cOnly 5 spots left for this week\u2019s strategy calls\u201d)    Highlight time-sensitive benefits (\u201cGet your valuation in 60 seconds\u201d)     \ud83e\uddea Real-World Example: Probate Landing Page    Here\u2019s a breakdown of a probate landing page we built for a client in Florida:    ElementExampleHeadline\u201cInheriting a Property? Get a Free Probate Home Valuation\u201dSubheadline\u201cWe\u2019ll help you understand your options \u2014 no pressure, no obligation.\u201dForm FieldsName, Email, Property AddressVisualPhoto of the agent + local courthouseTrust Signal\u201cOver 50 families helped with probate sales in \u201dCTA\u201cGet My Free Valuation\u201d    Conversion rate? 11.2% \u2014 and it generated 3 listings in the first 45 days.    \ud83d\udd17 External Resources     HubSpot: Anatomy of a Landing Page    Unbounce: Landing Page Design Principles    WordStream: 11 Elements of a High-Converting Landing Page     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Insight     \u201cA landing page is like a handshake. It\u2019s your first impression \u2014 and it better be strong, clear, and confident.\u201d         \ud83d\udea6 Traffic Strategies \u2014 How to Drive the Right People to Your Landing Page    \ud83e\udde0 The Truth About Traffic    Here\u2019s something I tell every agent and team we work with at MNKY.agency:     \"You don\u2019t have a lead problem \u2014 you have a traffic problem.\"     If your landing page isn\u2019t converting, it\u2019s usually not the page. It\u2019s the people you\u2019re sending to it. You need qualified, motivated, niche-specific traffic \u2014 and you need to meet them where they are.    Let\u2019s break down the most effective traffic sources for real estate landing pages, and how to use each one strategically.    \ud83d\udcb0 1. Paid Traffic (PPC & Social Ads)    \ud83d\udd25 Why It Works:    Paid traffic is fast, scalable, and highly targeted. You can get leads within hours of launching a campaign \u2014 if your targeting and messaging are dialed in.    \ud83d\udccd Best Platforms:     Facebook & Instagram Ads (great for buyers, sellers, and niche audiences)    Google Search Ads (great for high-intent leads like \u201csell my house fast in \u201d)    YouTube Ads (great for branding + retargeting)     \ud83e\uddf2 Landing Page Pairings:    Traffic SourceBest Landing Page TypeFacebook AdsBuyer, Seller, Probate, RelocationGoogle AdsHome Valuation, Probate, InvestorInstagram AdsLuxury, First-Time BuyerYouTube AdsWebinar, Relocation, Brand Story    \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cDon\u2019t send paid traffic to your homepage. Ever. Every ad should have its own landing page \u2014 and that page should match the ad\u2019s message exactly.\u201d     \ud83d\udd0d 2. Organic SEO    \ud83c\udf31 Why It Works:    SEO takes time, but it\u2019s free, sustainable, and high-converting. People who find you through search are often further down the funnel.    \ud83d\udccd Best Tactics:     Create geo-targeted landing pages (e.g., \u201cHome Valuation in Marbella\u201d)    Optimize for long-tail keywords (e.g., \u201chow to sell an inherited house in Andalusia\u201d)    Use schema markup for local SEO    Build backlinks from local directories and real estate blogs     \ud83e\uddf2 Landing Page Pairings:     Home Valuation    Probate    Relocation    Luxury     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cWe\u2019ve ranked landing pages on page 1 of Google just by optimizing the headline, meta description, and adding 500 words of localized content. SEO doesn\u2019t have to be complicated \u2014 it just has to be consistent.\u201d     \ud83d\udce7 3. Email Marketing    \ud83d\udcec Why It Works:    Email is personal, direct, and high-converting \u2014 especially when paired with a strong offer.    \ud83d\udccd Best Use Cases:     Send a weekly \u201chot list\u201d of properties    Offer a free guide or checklist    Re-engage cold leads with a new offer     \ud83e\uddf2 Landing Page Pairings:     Buyer Lists    Seller Strategy Sessions    Investment Deals    Relocation Guides     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cYour CRM is a goldmine. Don\u2019t just drip on your leads \u2014 give them something new to click on every month.\u201d     \ud83d\udd01 4. Period.\u201d     \ud83d\udcf1 5. That\u2019s where the magic happens.\u201d         \ud83c\udfaf Conversion Optimization \u2014 Getting More Leads from the Same Traffic    \ud83e\udde0 Why Optimization Matters    You\u2019ve built a beautiful landing page. You\u2019re driving traffic. But you\u2019re not getting the leads you expected. Sound familiar?     The truth is, most landing pages don\u2019t need more traffic \u2014 they need better conversion.     At MNKY.agency, we\u2019ve helped clients double or even triple their lead volume without increasing ad spend \u2014 just by optimizing the page itself.    Let\u2019s break down the most effective ways to boost your conversion rate.    \ud83d\udd01 1. A\/B Testing: The Secret Weapon    \ud83e\uddea What It Is:    A\/B testing (also called split testing) is the process of comparing two versions of a landing page to see which one performs better.    \ud83e\uddf2 What to Test:     Headlines: \u201cGet Your Home\u2019s Value\u201d vs. \u201cWhat\u2019s Your Home Worth in Marbella?\u201d    CTA Buttons: \u201cSubmit\u201d vs. \u201cGet My Free Report\u201d    Images: Stock photo vs. local neighborhood photo    Form Fields: 3 fields vs. 5 fields    Trust Signals: With vs. without testimonials     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cAlways be testing. Even a 1% lift in conversion can mean thousands of dollars in extra GCI over time.\u201d     \ud83d\udcf1 2. Mobile Optimization: Non-Negotiable    \ud83d\udcca Why It Matters:    Over 70% of real estate landing page traffic comes from mobile devices. If your page isn\u2019t mobile-friendly, you\u2019re losing leads.    \u2705 Best Practices:     Use large, tappable buttons    Keep forms short and scrollable    Optimize load speed (under 3 seconds)    Avoid pop-ups that block the screen     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cTest your landing page on your own phone. If it\u2019s clunky or slow, fix it \u2014 fast.\u201d     \ud83e\udde0 3. Psychological Triggers That Boost Conversions    \ud83e\uddf2 Social Proof     Add testimonials, reviews, or \u201cX people downloaded this guide\u201d counters    Use real names and photos when possible     \u23f3 Urgency     \u201cOnly 3 spots left for this week\u2019s seller strategy sessions\u201d    Countdown timers for events or limited offers     \ud83d\udd12 Trust & Security     \u201cWe respect your privacy. No spam, ever.\u201d    SSL certificate and secure form messaging     \ud83c\udf81 Reciprocity     Offer something valuable for free (guide, checklist, valuation)    Make it feel exclusive: \u201cOnly available to homeowners in \u201d     \ud83e\uddea Real-World Example: First-Time Buyer Page    We worked with an agent in Atlanta targeting first-time buyers. Her original page had a generic headline, a long form, and no testimonials. Conversion rate? 2.1%    We made three changes:     Changed the headline to: \u201cBuying Your First Home in Atlanta? Start Here.\u201d    Shortened the form to just name + email    Added a testimonial from a recent buyer     New conversion rate? 6.4% \u2014 a 3x improvement with zero extra ad spend.    \ud83d\udcca 4. Use Heatmaps & Analytics    \ud83d\udd0d Tools We Recommend:     Hotjar or Microsoft Clarity for heatmaps and session recordings    Google Analytics for bounce rate and time on page    Meta Pixel and Google Tag Manager for conversion tracking     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cWatch how people actually use your page. Where they scroll, where they click, where they drop off \u2014 it\u2019s like reading their mind.\u201d     \ud83e\uddf0 5. Follow-Up Systems: Don\u2019t Let Leads Go Cold    Even the best landing page won\u2019t close deals on its own. You need a follow-up system that nurtures leads until they\u2019re ready to act.    \ud83d\udd04 Best Practices:     Use an automated email drip (3\u20135 emails minimum)    Send a text within 5 minutes of form submission    Offer a calendar link for booking a call     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Tip:     \u201cSpeed to lead is everything. If you\u2019re not following up within 5 minutes, someone else is.\u201d     \ud83d\udd17 External Resources     CXL: A\/B Testing Guide    Hotjar: How to Use Heatmaps    NAR: Mobile Usage in Real Estate     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Insight     \u201cConversion optimization isn\u2019t a one-time thing \u2014 it\u2019s a mindset. Test, tweak, repeat. That\u2019s how you turn a good landing page into a lead-generating machine.\u201d         \ud83c\udfaf Real Estate Niches & Customization Tips    \ud83e\udde0 Why Niche-Specific Landing Pages Work    In real estate, context is everything. A probate lead has different needs than a luxury buyer. An investor wants numbers, not fluff. A first-time buyer wants guidance, not jargon.     The more specific your landing page, the more it resonates \u2014 and the better it converts.     At MNKY.agency, we\u2019ve built hundreds of niche-specific landing pages. Here\u2019s how to tailor your messaging, design, and offer for each one.    \ud83c\udfdb\ufe0f 1. Probate & Inherited Property    \ud83c\udfaf Audience:     Heirs, executors, and families managing estates    Often overwhelmed, emotional, and unfamiliar with the process     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:    \u201cFree Probate Home Valuation + Step-by-Step Guide to Selling an Inherited Property\u201d    \ud83e\udde0 Messaging Tips:     Use empathetic, non-salesy language    Focus on education and support    Avoid urgency or pressure tactics     \ud83d\udd27 Customization Ideas:     Include a downloadable probate checklist    Add a short video explaining the probate process    Use testimonials from families you\u2019ve helped     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Insight:     \u201cI\u2019ve worked with dozens of probate specialists. First-Time Homebuyers    \ud83c\udfaf Audience:     Millennials, Gen Z, renters, newlyweds    Often anxious, budget-conscious, and unsure where to start     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:    \u201cFree First-Time Buyer Guide + Access to Homes Under  in \u201d    \ud83e\udde0 Messaging Tips:     Be friendly, approachable, and educational    Use simple language \u2014 avoid industry jargon    Highlight affordability and financing options     \ud83d\udd27 Customization Ideas:     Include a mortgage calculator    Offer a downloadable \u201cFirst-Time Buyer Checklist\u201d    Add a FAQ section (\u201cWhat\u2019s a pre-approval?\u201d)     \ud83c\udfd7\ufe0f 3. Fix & Flip \/ Investor Leads    \ud83c\udfaf Audience:     Cash buyers, flippers, landlords    Data-driven, ROI-focused, fast-moving     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:    \u201cGet Weekly Off-Market Deals in  \u2014 Before They Hit the MLS\u201d    \ud83e\udde0 Messaging Tips:     Focus on numbers: ARV, cap rate, ROI    Use urgency and scarcity (\u201cOnly 3 deals left this week\u201d)    Keep it short and direct     \ud83d\udd27 Customization Ideas:     Include a sample deal breakdown    Add a \u201cDeal Analyzer\u201d download    Use a bold, no-nonsense design     \ud83c\udfd9\ufe0f 4. Luxury Buyers & Sellers    \ud83c\udfaf Audience:     High-net-worth individuals, executives, international buyers    Expect discretion, exclusivity, and premium service     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:    \u201cPrivate Access to Luxury Listings in  \u2014 Not Available on Zillow\u201d    \ud83e\udde0 Messaging Tips:     Use elegant, minimalist design    Highlight privacy, exclusivity, and white-glove service    Avoid aggressive CTAs \u2014 use \u201cRequest Access\u201d or \u201cSchedule a Private Consultation\u201d     \ud83d\udd27 Customization Ideas:     Include a video tour of a luxury property    Add a downloadable luxury market report    Use high-end photography and branding     \ud83e\uddf3 5. Relocation Clients    \ud83c\udfaf Audience:     Out-of-town buyers, corporate relocations, military families    Need local knowledge and logistical support     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:    \u201cFree Relocation Guide to  + Neighborhood Insights\u201d    \ud83e\udde0 Messaging Tips:     Position yourself as a local expert and guide    Include school info, commute times, and lifestyle highlights    Use welcoming, helpful language     \ud83d\udd27 Customization Ideas:     Add a downloadable relocation guide    Include a map of neighborhoods with pros\/cons    Offer a virtual consultation booking link     \ud83e\uddfe 6. FSBO & Expired Listings    \ud83c\udfaf Audience:     Sellers who tried to go it alone \u2014 and failed    Frustrated, skeptical, and wary of agents     \ud83e\uddf2 Offer:    \u201cWhy Your Home Didn\u2019t Sell \u2014 Free Report + Strategy Session\u201d    \ud83e\udde0 Messaging Tips:     Be empathetic, not critical    Focus on solutions and second chances    Use data to back up your claims     \ud83d\udd27 Customization Ideas:     Include a \u201cTop 5 Reasons Homes Don\u2019t Sell\u201d PDF    Add a testimonial from a former FSBO client    Offer a free pricing analysis     \ud83e\udde0 Stu\u2019s Insight    \u201cThe riches are in the niches. <span id="ib-toc-anchor-1"></span><h1 class="wp-block-heading"><strong>Real Estate Landing Pages That Convert: The Ultimate Guide for Agents & Brokerages</strong></h1>



<p><strong>Author:</strong> <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/j-stuart-hill/">J. Stuart Hill, CEO of MNKY.agency</a><br><strong>Estimated Read Time:</strong> 35–40 minutes</p>



<span id="ib-toc-anchor-2"></span><h2 class="wp-block-heading" id="1"><img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="🧠" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f9e0.svg"> TL;DR (Too Long; Didn’t Read)</h2>



<p>If you’re a real estate agent, team leader, or brokerage owner looking to generate more leads online, this guide is your blueprint. I’ve spent over two decades building real estate websites and lead generation systems for every niche imaginable — from probate and trustee sales to luxury listings and first-time homebuyers. And if there’s one thing I’ve learned, it’s this:</p>



<blockquote class="wp-block-quote is-layout-flow wp-block-quote-is-layout-flow">
<p><strong>Landing pages are the single most underutilized tool in real estate marketing.</strong></p>
</blockquote>



<p>In this guide, I’ll walk you through everything you need to know about real estate landing pages — what they are, why they work, how to build them, and how to get them in front of the right people. Whether you’re running Facebook ads, Google PPC, or just want to capture more organic leads, this is the playbook.</p>



<span id="ib-toc-anchor-3"></span><h3 class="wp-block-heading"><img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="🔑" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f511.svg"> Key Takeaways</h3>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>Landing pages convert better than websites.</strong> Period. They’re focused, distraction-free, and built for one thing: capturing leads.</li>



<li><strong>Every niche needs a different approach.</strong> A probate lead is not the same as a luxury buyer. Your landing pages should reflect that.</li>



<li><strong>Design matters.</strong> A beautiful, fast, mobile-optimized page builds trust and increases conversions.</li>



<li><strong>Traffic without strategy is wasted.</strong> You need the right ad copy, targeting, and follow-up systems to make landing pages work.</li>



<li><strong>MNKY.agency builds landing pages that work.</strong> We’ve helped hundreds of agents and teams generate thousands of leads — and we can do the same for you.</li>
</ul>



<span id="ib-toc-anchor-4"></span><h2 class="wp-block-heading"><img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="👋" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f44b.svg"> About the Author: Stu Hill, CEO of MNKY.agency</h2>



<p>I’ve been in the real estate marketing game since the early 2000s — back when IDX websites were cutting-edge and Craigslist was a lead source. Over the years, I’ve worked with agents in every niche you can think of:</p>



<ul class="wp-block-list">
<li><strong>Probate & inherited property</strong></li>



<li><strong>Trustee sales & short sales</strong></li>



<li><strong>Fix & flip investors</strong></li>



<li><strong>Luxury property marketing</strong></li>



<li><strong>First-time homebuyers</strong></li>



<li><strong>New construction & developments</strong></li>
</ul>



<p>I founded <strong>MNKY.agency</strong> to bring high-performance marketing to real estate professionals who are serious about growth. We don’t just build pretty websites — we build systems that generate leads, nurture them, and convert them into closings.</p>



<p>This guide is the culmination of everything I’ve learned about <strong>real estate landing pages</strong> — and how to use them to grow your business.</p>



<span id="ib-toc-anchor-5"></span><h2 class="wp-block-heading"><img draggable="false" role="img" class="emoji" alt="🧭" src="https://s.w.org/images/core/emoji/17.0.2/svg/1f9ed.svg"> What’s Inside This Guide</h2>
